commands.review_summary:
This PR adds an optional deliveryThreadId fallback to resolveRoutedDeliveryThreadId and passes preparedSessionState.sessionEntry.deliveryContext.threadId from runPreparedReply.

PR surface: Source +16. Total +16 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was established. Source inspection shows current main cannot read deliveryContext.threadId through resolveRoutedDeliveryThreadId for an ACP-style key, but no failing test or real ACP Discord run was provided.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor/maintainer proof for a live message-routing path, not a safe automated repair task.

target.title: fix: resolve ACP delivery threadId from session deliveryContext
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

ACP sessions (e.g. Discord delivery via ACP) fail to route replies because `resolveRoutedDeliveryThreadId` cannot extract a `threadId` from ACP session keys. The function's three-tier fallback chain ã `MessageThreadId` ã `TransportThreadId` ã `parseSessionThreadInfoFast` ã all return nothing because:

1. `MessageThreadId` and `TransportThreadId` are null when delivering through the ACP coordinator (the delivery is not an inbound message)
2. `parseSessionThreadInfoFast` parses thread info from the session key suffix (`:thread:...`) but ACP session keys use the format `agent:<id>:acp:<uuid>` which has no `:thread:` suffix

However, the session entry's `deliveryContext.threadId` IS set during ACP spawn (`src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts` adds it via `requestDeliveryHint`), and `preparedSessionState.sessionEntry` is already available at the call site in `get-reply-run.ts`. The data was there ã it just wasn't being used.

## Why This Change Was Made

The root cause is in `resolveRoutedDeliveryThreadId` (`src/auto-reply/reply/routed-delivery-thread.ts`): it has no awareness of a session's `deliveryContext.threadId`. The fix adds a `deliveryThreadId` parameter that slots between the inbound thread id checks and the session-key parse:

```
MessageThreadId ã TransportThreadId ã deliveryThreadId (NEW) ã parseSessionThreadInfoFast
```

This is placed before `parseSessionThreadInfoFast` because `deliveryContext.threadId` is authoritatively set by the gateway during session/spawn and is more reliable than parsing a session key convention.

Only the `get-reply-run.ts` call site (which computes `originatingThreadId`) is wired up, because it's the entry point that feeds thread context into `createAcpDispatchDeliveryCoordinator`. The `dispatch-from-config.ts` call site is intentionally not changed ã it has a comment warning that reading from the normalised session store's `deliveryContext` can resurrect stale routes after thread delivery was intentionally cleared.

## Evidence

### Existing tests pass

```
$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts --no-coverage
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  31 passed (31)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts --no-coverage -t "thread"
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  6 passed | 217 skipped (223)
```

The existing `resolveRoutedDeliveryThreadId("uses session key thread id suffix")` test continues to pass ã the new `deliveryThreadId` parameter is optional and doesn't affect callers that don't pass it.

### Not tested

- End-to-end ACP Discord delivery with a real Discord channel (requires live Discord + agent infrastructure not available in this environment)
- The fix is mechanically correct: it reads a field that the gateway already writes and passes it through the same type-safe `string | number` path used by the existing fallbacks

## Merge Risk

Low. The change:
- Adds one optional parameter (no existing callers broken)
- Reads `deliveryContext?.threadId` from an already-loaded session entry (no new IO)
- Uses the same `string | number` type already supported by `resolveRoutedDeliveryThreadId`

ÞÊ Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
